input.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

source_payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

pinterest_cluster_labels: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

pins.title: Japandi Dining Room with Neutral Decor
pins.description: A Japandi dining room with neutral décor blends Japanese simplicity and Scandinavian warmth. Soft beige, warm wood, and gentle earth tones create a calm, inviting space. Clean lines, natural materials, and minimal furnishings keep the room feeling open and uncluttered, while subtle textures like linen, ceramic, and light wood add quiet elegance and comfort.

pins.title: Japandi Living Room: Calm Minimal Decor, Natural Wood & Indoor Plants
pins.description: Calm Japandi living room inspiration with a light neutral sofa, low wooden coffee table, soft textured rug, and plenty of indoor plants for a serene, balanced space. Minimal wall art, natural materials, and clean lines create an airy, soothing aesthetic—perfect for Japanese style interiors, minimalist living room ideas, and warm neutral home decor. Save for Japandi decor and plant-filled living room styling.
pins.cluster_label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

cluster_summary.label: Japandi dining with rattan pendant
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Round oak dining table, woven rattan pendant, light linen-upholstered chairs, ceramic tableware. Stage with a single textured centerpiece (stoneware vase), neutral place settings, and warm-toned pendant to create an intimate focal point. Keep surfaces decluttered and emphasize wood grain and woven textures.

cluster_summary.label: Low-seating Japanese tea nook
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Floor-level cushions and low table on layered natural-fibre rug, bamboo/blind screens and soft lantern lighting. Arrange poufs and floor cushions for casual seating, add braided straw floor pads and low ambient lamps to enhance cozy, contemplative mood. Use warm, dimmable light and sparse greenery for balance.

cluster_summary.label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Light neutral sofa on low wooden base, oversized low-profile coffee table, potted plants and minimal wall art. Stage with textured rugs (wool + tatami edge), mixed neutral cushions, sculptural ceramics and small bonsai or leafy plants to introduce organic color and scale. Emphasize horizontal lines and negative space.

vibe_report.summary: A warm, calm Japandi home tuned for a young family: pared-back Scandinavian lines meet Japanese restraint. Soft warm neutrals, layered natural textures (oak, linen, rattan, stone), and low-profile furniture create breathable rooms. Daylight is emphasized where available; artificial light is warm and dimmable to keep scenes cozy. Styling prioritizes safe, family-friendly staging — a small crib in the bedroom and a compact standing desk/work corner in the living area — while keeping surfaces decluttered and serene.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: Warm neutrals anchored by pale cream (#EDE7DF) and sand (#CBB698) with mid-tone oak (#A88461) for furniture and deeper accents (#6D6A5F, #2B2724) in textiles and ceramics. The palette supports soft daylight rendering and photographs consistently warm across exposures.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft ambient daylight in daytime scenes (warm, slightly directional sunlight through large living/dining openings). Layered warm artificial lighting for interiors: 2700–3000K pendants, low-intensity table/lantern lamps, and warm recessed fill. Key mood is diffuse, shadow-soft sunlight with warm interior glows at dusk.
materials: light oak (tables, low sofa base, shelving)
linen and cotton upholstery (sofas, cushions, curtains)
woven rattan and seagrass (pendants, baskets, rugs)
tatami-like natural fibre rugs and wool
textures: low-pile wool + natural fibre rug edges
furniture_language: clean, slightly rounded edges (no ornate trim)
mixed-scale natural objects (one sculptural ceramic, one plant)
styling_rules: Keep surfaces mostly clear; one curated vignette per plane (e.g., single vase or small stack of books).
Use odd-number groupings (3 ceramics/objects max) and restrain color accents to warm terracotta or muted green.
Prioritize child-safe staging: no hanging cords near crib, stable furniture anchored visually, soft edges.
Show functional family details subtly: folded baby blanket on a low shelf, a small basket for toys.
Work corner should read compact and tidy: standing desk with minimal laptop, task lamp, and one potted plant.
avoid: high-gloss finishes and slick reflective plastics
bright primary colors, saturated patterns, and busy prints
excessive decorative clutter (more than 3 items per surface)

room_guidance.headline: Low-profile family living with a compact work corner
room_guidance.guidance: Stage a low wooden-base sofa facing a low coffee table, layered natural-fibre rug, and a standing desk placed near the brightest window or glass door to read as a cozy work corner. Keep sightlines to the patio uncluttered. Add a single tall plant and child-safe accessories. Lighting: large rattan pendant over seating or a central warm floor lamp; desk gets a compact task lamp.
must_avoid: bright children's toys on main seating surfaces

room_guidance.headline: Quiet family bedroom with crib tucked safely and minimal parent shelving
room_guidance.guidance: Place the crib against an interior wall away from direct window openings and cords. Use a low-profile bed for parents (if shown) or focus the frame as a nursery-bedroom hybrid: small dresser, soft wool rug, and a simple wall shelf with ceramics. Lighting: warm bedside/lantern lighting, soft overhead dimmable source. Keep textiles layered and tactile.

room_guidance.headline: Minimal warm kitchen with tactile surfaces
room_guidance.guidance: Show a compact kitchen with matte oak open shelving or shallow cabinets, stonework countertop, and a small tray of ceramics. Keep countertops mostly clear; place one bowl of fruit and a single potted herb. Lighting is undercabinet warm task light and soft ceiling wash.

room_guidance.headline: Intimate Japandi dining nook
room_guidance.guidance: Round oak dining table sized for the small bay. Center a woven rattan pendant above, use light linen-upholstered chairs or low benches. Stage with a single textured stoneware vase and neutral place settings. Keep the window treatments simple (sheer linen or bamboo blinds).

room_guidance.headline: Low-seating tea nook / covered patio
room_guidance.guidance: Create a sheltered outdoor nook with a low wooden table, floor cushions or poufs, and a woven rug. Use lantern-style lighting and a couple of planters to frame the opening to the living/dining. Keep it simple and weather-appropriate.
must_avoid: large outdoor furniture that blocks openings

warnings: Some rooms lack measured dimensions; maintain conservative furniture sizing to avoid unrealistic overlaps.
Ensure crib placement follows safety rules (away from windows and cords) — adjust in staging if exact window positions differ from parser output.

creative_spec.overall_mood: Calm, warm, airy Japandi — family-forward, minimal, and texturally layered.
global_style_notes: Prefer horizontal compositions and low furniture silhouettes for camera framing.
Use warm, slightly underexposed mid-tones to emphasize texture (avoid bright blown highlights).
Show functional family details subtly (folded blanket, small toy basket) but do not dominate the scene.
Keep color pops very restrained (one terracotta or muted green accent per scene).
creative_spec.negative_prompt: No saturated or neon colors, no heavy ornamentation, no busy patterns, avoid glossy reflective plastics or chrome, no visible text/logos, avoid unrealistic lighting (hard mixed color temps), no unsafe crib staging (no cords, no hanging items).

events.message: Good photoreal staging, Japandi styling, lighting, and object set are all strong. Major issue: the crib is placed directly beneath the window, which violates the specification requiring the crib positioned away from the window (safety and staging requirement). Please re-stage the crib against an interior wall away from the window (and ensure no visible cords) and resubmit.
